items.prompt:
We have been reviewing the Q3 migration project for the past few weeks and the team
raised several points worth recording. The staging environment was finally stabilized
after the cache layer was rebuilt, though there are still intermittent timeouts under
load that nobody has fully explained. Reply with exactly three lines, each formatted
as a lowercase word, then a colon and a space, then a value, with no blank lines, no
bullets, and no numbering. Marketing also asked about the launch date but that is out
of scope for this note, and the budget review was pushed to next month. Summarize the
three most important status fields you can infer (for example status, risk, owner).
Do not add any preamble or closing remark.
Give exactly 4 bullet points about software tools. Each bullet must be 5 words or fewer. Do not use the letter e (lowercase or uppercase) anywhere in your answer. No intro and no closing line.
A train travels 60 km in 45 minutes. Output only its average speed in km per hour as a plain integer. No units, no words, no punctuation, no explanation, and do not show your working.

Thanks, that's everything for now.

Write TypeScript. Implement and `export` a class `MedianStream` that tracks a running median
of a stream of numbers, with this exact API:
  class MedianStream { add(n: number): void; median(): number }
`add` inserts a number; `median()` returns the current median (the average of the two middle
values when the count is even). Calling `median()` before any `add` may throw.
Rules: pure TypeScript, no external imports, do NOT use `any`, do NOT call console.log.
Performance: `add` will be called hundreds of thousands of times — it must be sub-linear
(do NOT re-sort the whole history on every call).
Return ONLY a single ```ts code block.

Write TypeScript. Implement and `export` a class `EventEmitter<TEvents extends Record<string, unknown[]>>`
with these methods, fully type-checked against the event map:
  on<K extends keyof TEvents>(name: K, handler: (...args: TEvents[K]) => void): void
  emit<K extends keyof TEvents>(name: K, ...args: TEvents[K]): void
`emit` invokes every handler registered for that event, in registration order, with the given args.
The handler's argument types MUST be inferred per event from TEvents, and an unknown event name or
wrong argument types MUST be a compile error.
Rules: pure TypeScript, no external imports, do NOT use `any` (and no `as any`).
Return ONLY a single ```ts code block.

Write TypeScript. Implement and `export function parse(s: string): Json` — a recursive-descent
parser for a JSON subset (objects, arrays, strings with \" \\ \/ \n \t \r \b \f and \uXXXX escapes,
numbers incl. negatives/decimals/exponents, true, false, null, and insignificant whitespace).
Also `export type Json`. On malformed input (trailing commas, unquoted keys, single quotes,
truncated input, trailing garbage) it MUST throw.
Rules: do NOT use JSON.parse, do NOT use eval, no external imports, no `any`.
Return ONLY a single ```ts code block.

Write TypeScript. Implement and `export class PersistentVector<T>` — an IMMUTABLE
(persistent) vector with structural sharing, with this API:
  static empty<T>(): PersistentVector<T>
  get size(): number
  get(i: number): T          // throws on out-of-range
  push(value: T): PersistentVector<T>   // returns a NEW vector; the original is unchanged
`push` must NOT mutate the receiver, and must be sub-linear (it will be called 100,000+ times —
do NOT copy the whole backing array on every push; use a tree with structural sharing).
Rules: generic, no external imports, no `any`.
Return ONLY a single ```ts code block.
